incident.event_summary: The `api-backend` host (10.128.0.30) retrieved container images from Google Artifact Registry over HTTPS, using a self-signed staging certificate issued by Let's Encrypt.
raw_logs: The `api-backend` host (10.128.0.30) retrieved container images from Google Artifact Registry over HTTPS, using a self-signed staging certificate issued by Let's Encrypt.
The `ids-vm` (10.128.0.50) sent an ICMP ping to the `web-frontend` (10.128.0.20) as part of routine packet mirroring integrity checks.
A unit-test pod in the GKE cluster (10.128.0.35) transmitted a non-functional Google API key during automated security scanning validation.
The `ids-vm` (10.128.0.50) performed a vulnerability scan on the `api-backend` (10.128.0.30) as part of scheduled security assessments.
An SSH connection was established from the `bastion-vm` (10.128.0.5) to the `web-frontend` (10.128.0.20) for administrative purposes.
The `api-backend` (10.128.0.30) accessed a service using Base64-encoded HTTP basic authentication, as expected for certain API interactions.
The `web-frontend` (10.128.0.20) performed a DNS query to Google’s public DNS resolver (8.8.8.8) for cloud service operations.
The unit-test pod (10.128.0.35) accessed Google Cloud Storage over HTTPS as part of a scripted test workflow.
The `bastion-vm` (10.128.0.5) broadcasted a Windows Active Directory domain controller announcement within the subnet.
The unit-test pod (10.128.0.35) accessed the Kubernetes API (10.128.0.1) for cluster administration tasks.
The `api-backend` (10.128.0.30) performed a scheduled backup verification with Google Cloud Storage.
The external load balancer (34.118.90.120) conducted an HTTP health check on the `web-frontend` (10.128.0.20).
The `ids-vm` (10.128.0.50) initiated an internal vulnerability scan on the `web-frontend` (10.128.0.20) as part of routine security checks.
